title: crenularia polygaloides

These constituents include quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, thereby improving wound healing, reducing the risk of chronic diseases, and promoting overall well-being. parts: Crenularia polygaloides has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The roots of Crenularia polygaloides are rich in alkaloids and terpenoids, with constituents like berberine and palmarine exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Crenularia polygaloides have been found to contain saponins and glycosides, with constituents like astragaloside and polygaloside demonstrating immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Crenularia polygaloides can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea and vomiting due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to kidney damage and failure. Additionally, improper use of the plant has been linked to skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering.
